evidences.title: EDT to UTC Converter - World Time Buddy
evidences.content: | Converting EDT to UTC This time zone converter lets you visually and very quickly convert EDT to UTC and vice-versa. Simply mouse over the colored hour-tiles and glance at the hours selected by the column... and done! EDT stands for Eastern Daylight Time. UTC is known as Universal Time. UTC is 4 hours ahead of EDT. So, when it is 12:00 am EDT1:00 am EDT2:00 am EDT3:00 am EDT4:00 am EDT5:00 am EDT6:00 am EDT7:00 am EDT8:00 am EDT9:00 am EDT10:00 am EDT11:00 am EDT12:00 pm EDT1:00 pm EDT2:00 pm EDT3:00 pm EDT4:00 pm EDT5:00 pm EDT6:00 pm EDT7:00 pm EDT8:00 pm EDT9:00 pm EDT10:00 pm EDT11:00 pm EDT it will be 4:00 am UTC5:00 am UTC6:00 am UTC7:00 am UTC8:00 am UTC9:00 am UTC10:00 am UTC11:00 am UTC12:00 pm UTC1:00 pm UTC2:00 pm UTC3:00 pm UTC4:00 pm UTC5:00 pm UTC6:00 pm UTC7:00 pm [...] | Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) to Universal Time (UTC) | | | --- | 12 pm EDT | is | 4 pm UTC | | 1 pm EDT | is | 5 pm UTC | | 2 pm EDT | is | 6 pm UTC | | 3 pm EDT | is | 7 pm UTC | | 4 pm EDT | is | 8 pm UTC | | 5 pm EDT | is | 9 pm UTC | | 6 pm EDT | is | 10 pm UTC | | 7 pm EDT | is | 11 pm UTC | | 8 pm EDT | is | 12 am UTC | | 9 pm EDT | is | 1 am UTC | | 10 pm EDT | is | 2 am UTC | | 11 pm EDT...

evidences.content: EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM 4-hour difference during summer International Coordination EST Midnight → UTC 5:00 AM EDT Midnight → UTC 4:00 AM UTC used for global scheduling Technical Applications Server logs → UTC timestamps API calls → UTC standards UTC preferred for technical systems #### Conversion Tips & Best Practices • Add 5 hours to EST (winter) or 4 hours to EDT (summer) to get UTC • UTC is the preferred standard for international business, aviation, and technical applications • UTC is also known as "Zulu Time" in military and aviation contexts • For practical purposes, UTC and GMT are the same, though UTC is the modern standard • Always use 24-hour format when expressing UTC time (no AM/PM) [...] Time Difference: UTC is 5 hours ahead of EST (winter) and 4 hours ahead of EDT (summer). evidences.content: Converter supports daylight saving time (DST) or summer time, if the target location is currently using DST time zone, converter will automatically calculate and display local summer time. [...] Based International Air Transport Association (IATA) timezone codes. Time zones use hours and minutes offset from Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), most a whole number of hours, a few are offset by 30 or 45 minutes. Eastern Standard Time (EST) is UTC-5:00, and Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) is UTC-4:00, this time zone is called Eastern Time Zone (ET) in the United States, parts of eastern Canada, Mexico, Panama and the Caribbean Islands. Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) is the primary time standard now, time zones around the world are expressed using offsets from UTC, UTC offset is the difference in hours and minutes from UTC, a time zone can be determined by adding or subtracting the number of UTC offset. evidences.content: ### What is the difference between EST and EDT? EDT (Eastern Daylight Time) is used during Daylight Saving time and is UTC-4 hours. EST (Eastern Standard Time) is used outside of Daylight Saving Time and is UTC-5 hours. ### What are some major industries in the EST time zone? Copied embed code to clipboard! ## Frequently Asked Questions ### Is UTC always 4 hours behind of EST? Eastern Standard Time (EST) is usually 5 hours behind UTC, except during Daylight Saving Time, when it is 4 hours behind ### Is UTC -5 the same as EST? EST is the same as UTC -5, unless observing Daylight Saving Time, then it becomes UTC -4 ### What is EST? Eastern Standard Time (EST) is the easternmost timezone in the United States ### What is the difference between EST and EDT?

UTC is 5 hours ahead of EST or 4 hours ahead of EDT ### What is the time difference between Eastern Time and UTC? The time difference between Eastern Time and Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) depends on whether Daylight Saving Time is in effect. During standard time (EST), UTC is 5 hours ahead. During daylight time (EDT), UTC is 4 hours ahead.

evidences.title: Extract Historical Data from Binance Using Python
evidences.content: from binance.client import Clientimport pandas as pdimport datetime# Binance API credentialsapi_key = 'your_api_key'api_secret = 'your_api_secret'client = Client(api_key, api_secret)# Define the symbol for BTC/USDT pairsymbol = 'BTCUSDT'# Define custom start and end timestart_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 3, 15, 0, 0, 0)end_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 15, 0, 0, 0)klines = client.get_historical_klines(symbol=symbol, interval=Client.KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE, start_str=str(start_time), end_str=str(end_time))# Convert the data into a pandas dataframe for easier manipulationdf_M = pd.DataFrame(klines, columns=['Open Time', 'Open', 'High', 'Low', 'Close', 'Volume', 'Close Time', 'Quote Asset Volume', 'Number of Trades', 'Taker Buy Base Asset Volume', 'Taker Buy Quote Asset Volume', [...] ``` symbol = 'BTCUSDT' 'BTCUSDT' ``` We can set a custom start and end time for our function by parsing the date to date time format. ``` # format (year, month, day, hour, minute, second)start_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 3, 15, 0, 0, 0)end_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 15, 0, 0, 0) # format (year, month, day, hour, minute, second) 2024 3 15 0 0 0 2024 6 15 0 0 0 ``` `datetime.datetime`: Cr...

evidences.title: Binance API for Historical Crypto Prices | by Zachary Raicik - Medium
evidences.content: In this post, we focus on pulling prices from the `klines` endpoint. The Klines endpoint (/api/v3/klines) is a market data endpoint provided by the Binance API that allows developers to retrieve historical klines/candlestick chart data for a specific symbol (i.e., cryptocurrency) and interval. A `kline` is a representation of a fixed time interval of an asset’s price data, commonly used to represent the opening price, closing price, highest price, and lowest price for a given time period. The Klines endpoint allows developers to retrieve klines data for a variety of time intervals, such as 1 minute, 3 minutes, 5 minutes, 15 minutes, 30 minutes, 1 hour, 2 hours, 4 hours, 6 hours, 8 hours, 12 hours, and 1 day. ## API Keys [...] 1000), "endTime": int(dt.datetime.timestamp(request_end_date) 1000), "limit": 1000, } response = make_api_request(URL, params, headers) if response is not None: json_data = convert_to_json(response.json()) response_list = response_list + json_data request_start_date = request_end_date + datetime.timedelta(days=1) request_end_date = min( request_start_date + datetime.timedelta(days=number_of_days_per_request), datetime.datetime.combine(datetime.datetime.toda...

evidences.title: Binance API — python-binance 0.2.0 documentation
evidences.content: futures\_continuous\_klines(\\params)(_modules/binance/client.html#Client.futures_continuous_klines) : Kline/candlestick bars for a specific contract type. Klines are uniquely identified by their open time. futures\_countdown\_cancel\_all(\\params)(_modules/binance/client.html#Client.futures_countdown_cancel_all) : Cancel all open orders of the specified symbol at the end of the specified countdown. evidences.content: ### Klines​ `{ "id": "1dbbeb56-8eea-466a-8f6e-86bdcfa2fc0b", "method": "klines", "params": { "symbol": "BNBBTC", "interval": "1h", "startTime": 1655969280000, "limit": 1 } }` Get klines (candlestick bars). Klines are uniquely identified by their open & close time. If you need access to real-time kline updates, please consider using WebSocket Streams: `<symbol>@kline_<interval>` If you need historical kline data, please consider using data.binance.vision. evidences.title: Klines | Binance Open Platform
evidences.content: ## Full URL Example​ ## Parameters​ | Name | Type | Mandatory | Description | --- --- | | symbol | STRING | YES | e.g., "ALPHA\_175USDT" – use token ID from Token List | | interval | STRING | YES | e.g., "1h" – supported intervals: 1s, 15s, 1m, 3m, 5m, 15m, 30m, 1h, 2h, 4h, 6h, 8h, 12h, 1d, 3d, 1w, 1M | | limit | INT | NO | default 500, max 1500 – number of klines to return | | startTime | LONG | NO | start timestamp in milliseconds | | endTime | LONG | NO | end timestamp in milliseconds | ## Response Structure​ `code` `message` `messageDetail` `success` `data` ## Response Example​ [...] Binance Logo Binance Logo # Klines (Candlestick Data) ## API Description​ Fetches Kline/candlestick bars for a symbol, which include open/high/low/close prices and volume over intervals. Useful for charting and analysis. ## HTTP Request​ GET `/bapi/defi/v1/public/alpha-trade/klines` `/bapi/defi/v1/public/alpha-trade/klines` ## Full URL Example​ ## Parameters​ [...] ## Response Example​ `{ "code": "000000", "message": "", "messageDetail": "", "success": true, "data": [ [ "1752642000000", "0.00171473", "0.00172515", "0.00171473", "0.00172515", "1771.86000000", "1752645599999", "3.05093481", "2", "...

evidences.title: Binance.US API Documentation: Introduction
evidences.content: Data Source: Memory ### Get Candlestick Data > Example ``` -X "GET"" ``` ``` import requests resp = requests. get(' print(resp. json()) ``` > Response ``` ``` `GET /api/v3/klines` Use this endpoint to get Kline/candlestick bars for a token symbol. Klines are uniquely identified by their open time. Please note the maximum limit is 1,000 bars. Weight: 1 Parameters: | Name | Type | Mandatory | Description | --- --- | | symbol | STRING | YES | | interval | ENUM | YES | | startTime | LONG | NO | | endTime | LONG | NO | | limit | INT | NO | Default 500; max 1000 | If startTime and endTime are not sent, the most recent klines are returned. Data Source: Database ## Price Data ### Get Live Ticker Price > Example [...] secret_key = "NhqPtmdSJYdKjVHjA7PZj4Mge3R5YNiP1e3UZjInClVN65XAbvqqM6A7H5fATj0j" uri_path ="/api/v3/openOrders" data ={"symbol": "BTCUSDT", "timestamp": 1499827319559} get_open_order_result = binanceus_request(uri_path, data, api_key, secret_key) [...] `OCO` Order types (orderTypes, type): `LIMIT` `MARKET` `STOP_LOSS_LIMIT` `TAKE_PROFIT_LIMIT` `LIMIT_MAKER` Order side (side): `BUY` `SELL` Time in force (timeInForce): | Status | Description | --- | | `GTC` | "Good Till Cancel...

evidences.title: Binace API, Python and Close Price : r/quant - Reddit
evidences.content: # Binace API, Python and Close Price Hi guys! I need help understanding how closing prices work in real-time. In context: I use the Binance API in Python, analyzing closing prices in 30-minute windows, for this, I work with 1-minute intervals. I have a "while True" in my code that is constantly requesting through the "futures\_continous\_klines" module, limit windows of 30. When I'm running the code in real-time, the last value of the closing price always fluctuates according to each loop do while (which was to be expected).
evidences.query: Binance API klines BTCUSDT 1m historical endpoint get close for specific datetime
